steps.action: Bump Angular and Angular CLI packages (@angular/core, common, forms, platform-browser, router and CLI/build tooling) from ^18.2.0 to ^21 and run the official `ng update @angular/core @angular/cli` migrations; verify the workspace builds green.

Migrate all templates from structural directives (*ngFor/*ngIf) to built-in control-flow blocks: @for (with mandatory track) and @if/@switch. Drop the now-unneeded CommonModule import from each component's imports array.

Confirm ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush is set on every standalone component and convert any remaining mutable/view state to signal()/computed(); keep date rendering via TzDatePipe. No HttpClient calls move out of the typed core/api services.
Update existing component specs to assert the migrated control-flow output and signal-based state so coverage on changed files stays >=80%.

Update the 'Add an Angular feature' skill and CODING_STANDARDS to mandate Angular v21, signals/computed for state, OnPush on every component, and @if/@for/@switch control-flow blocks (replacing references to *ngIf/*ngFor).

Add a Jest e2e spec that boots TasksModule via Nest Test + supertest (mirroring the existing priorities e2e harness with a whitelist ValidationPipe) and verifies the task API contract the upgraded frontend depends on remains byte-for-byte unchanged: POST /tasks returns 201 with {id,title,completed:false,createdAt UTC ISO}; GET /tasks returns 200 array of Task shape; PATCH /tasks/:id/complete returns 200 with completed:true; DELETE /tasks/:id returns 200 {ok:true} then GET /tasks/:id returns 404; POST /tasks with empty/missing title returns 400 and creates nothing. No production backend code is modified.
